user.title: PowerSync setup
user.description: Configure PowerSync to sync electricity pricing to your battery system automatically.

provider_selection.title: Electricity provider
provider_selection.description: Select your electricity retailer. PowerSync will fetch pricing and sync it to your battery on a 5-minute cycle.

data_description.electricity_provider: If your retailer is not listed, choose Other / Custom TOU to enter rates manually.

agl.title: AGL Battery Rewards
agl.description: PowerSync models AGL as a static retail tariff. Enter the total feed-in rates from your current offer; the evening rate applies every day from 17:00 to 21:00. AGL VPP events are separate and are not controlled by this supplier integration.

data_description.agl_battery_rewards_peak_export_rate: Total export rate, not a bonus added to the off-peak rate. AGL currently advertises 28c/kWh; confirm your offer.
data_description.agl_battery_rewards_offpeak_export_rate: Export rate outside 17:00-21:00. AGL currently advertises 3c/kWh; confirm your offer.

covau_postcode.title: CovaU SolarMax service area
covau_postcode.description: Enter the supply postcode to filter the current fixture-backed SolarMax plans. You will confirm the distributor and exact AER plan next.

covau_plan.title: CovaU SolarMax plan and settlement meters
covau_plan.description: Confirm the exact plan and distributor from your bill. PowerSync caches the public AER/CDR snapshot and settles daily allowances only from the selected connection-point import and export energy meters.

data_description.covau_import_energy_entity: Preferred: select a monotonic total_increasing connection-point import energy sensor in kWh. Leave blank only to use continuous power-integrated estimation.
data_description.covau_export_energy_entity: Preferred: select a monotonic total_increasing connection-point export energy sensor in kWh. Leave blank only to use continuous power-integrated estimation.

covau_manual_tariff.title: Manual CovaU SolarMax tariff
covau_manual_tariff.description: Validated fallback for a withdrawn or account-specific SolarMax plan. Enter rates in cents per kWh and the daily supply charge in cents. The tariff clock remains fixed AEST (UTC+10).

flow_power_setup.title: Flow Power pricing
flow_power_setup.description: Enter your base rate and network tariff from your Flow Power bill.

data_description.flow_power_state: Your NEM region determines which wholesale price feed is used. Check your bill if unsure.
data_description.network_tariff_combined: Select the network tariff that matches your electricity bill.
data_description.flow_power_base_rate: The base energy rate from your Flow Power contract, before wholesale adjustments.
data_description.flowpower_api_key: Optional for AEMO Direct; required for Flow Power API prices and account metrics. Get it from the Flow Power app under More > Web Data Access.

flow_power_site.title: Flow Power site
flow_power_site.description: Select the residential NMI PowerSync should use for Flow Power API account metrics.

data_description.flowpower_nmi: PowerSync uses this site for Flow Power account metrics and tariff hints.

flow_power_tariff.title: Network tariff (v2 formula)
flow_power_tariff.description: Configure your DNSP network tariff for the corrected PEA formula with GST, network charges, and daily averaging. Select Skip to use the legacy formula.

data_description.skip_tariff: Check this to skip the v2 tariff setup and use the simpler legacy PEA formula instead.
data_description.fp_network: Your local network distributor (DNSP). Filtered by your NEM region.
data_description.fp_tariff_code: The tariff code from your electricity bill (e.g. RESELE, 6900).

amber.title: Amber Electric API key
amber.description: Connect your Amber account so PowerSync can read live pricing. Prices update every 5 minutes.

data_description.amber_api_token: Found in the Amber app under Profile > Developer > API key. Or visit [{amber_url}]({amber_url}).

amber_site_selection.title: Select your Amber site
amber_site_selection.description: Your Amber account has multiple sites. Select the NMI associated with this location.

data_description.amber_site_id: Choose the NMI at this address. Closed sites will not receive price updates.

localvolts.title: Localvolts API credentials
localvolts.description: Connect your Localvolts account for real-time 5-minute NEM wholesale pricing.

data_description.localvolts_api_key: Contact Localvolts support to request your API key. Keep it secret -- treat it like a password.
data_description.localvolts_partner_id: Your Localvolts Partner ID, provided alongside the API key.
data_description.localvolts_nmi: Your 10-digit National Meter Identifier (on your electricity bill). Enter * to authorize all NMIs on the account.

amber_settings.title: Amber pricing options
amber_settings.description: Configure spike protection, export boost, and chip mode for Amber pricing.

data_description.spike_protection_enabled: When Amber detects a price spike, artificially inflate buy prices to prevent your battery from charging from grid.
data_description.forecast_discrepancy_alert: Send a mobile notification when Amber predicted and High (higher-price) forecasts diverge significantly. Indicates the forecast spread is large and conservative scheduling may be preferable.
data_description.forecast_discrepancy_threshold: Average price difference between Amber predicted and High forecasts that triggers the alert. Default 10 in the selected price unit.
data_description.force_tariff_mode_toggle: After uploading a new tariff, briefly toggle operation mode to force immediate recalculation. Tesla only, causes a ~5 second interruption.
data_description.export_boost_enabled: Artificially increase sell prices during configured hours to encourage battery export during high-value periods.
data_description.export_price_offset: Amount added to the actual Amber export price. Example: Amber 18c + Offset 5c = battery sees 23c.
data_description.export_min_price: Floor price after offset. Example: Amber 12c + Offset 5c = 17c, bumped to Floor 20c.
data_description.export_boost_start: Start time for the export boost window (24-hour format).
data_description.export_boost_end: End time for the export boost window (24-hour format).
data_description.export_boost_threshold: Only apply boost when the actual Amber price (before boost) is at or above this value. Use 0 to always boost.
data_description.chip_mode_enabled: Suppress exports during configured hours unless price exceeds threshold. The inverse of export boost.
data_description.chip_mode_start: Start time for the chip mode window (24-hour format).
data_description.chip_mode_end: End time for the chip mode window (24-hour format).
data_description.chip_mode_threshold: Allow exports only when the price is at or above this value during chip mode hours.

aemo_config.title: AEMO spike detection
aemo_config.description:
When AEMO wholesale prices spike above your threshold, PowerSync will switch your battery to export mode to earn spike credits.

{threshold_hint}

data_description.aemo_spike_enabled: Required for Globird and AEMO VPP plans that include spike export payments. Disable if your plan does not pay for spike exports.
data_description.aemo_region: Your NEM region determines which AEMO price feed is monitored. Check your electricity bill if unsure.
data_description.aemo_spike_threshold: Battery exports when the AEMO wholesale price exceeds this value. Default 3,000 in the selected market price unit matches most Globird and VPP plans. Only change if your plan specifies a different threshold.

globird_plan.title: GloBird plan
globird_plan.description: Select your exact GloBird plan. ZeroHero and ZeroCharge terms vary by account and date, so choose the preset that matches your written GloBird plan.

data_description.globird_plan: Choose ZeroHero Jul 2026 (10c, 15 kWh, 18:00-21:00, free 12:00-15:00), an older ZeroHero preset, a custom/account-specific setup, or Not on ZeroHero. Existing saved plans are not migrated automatically.
data_description.globird_zerohero_start: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom.
data_description.globird_zerohero_end: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom.
data_description.globird_zerohero_export_cap_kwh: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom. This is the daily kWh cap that receives the Super Export rate.
data_description.globird_zerohero_super_export_rate: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom. This is the total export rate, not an amount added to the base feed-in tariff.
data_description.globird_zerohero_credit_amount: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom.
data_description.globird_zerohero_import_limit_kw: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom. Jul 2026 uses 0.03 kWh per hour over 18:00-21:00, giving a 0.09 kWh total import allowance for the evening credit.
data_description.globird_zerocharge_start: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom. Jul 2026 free import starts at 12:00.
data_description.globird_zerocharge_end: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom. Jul 2026 free import ends at 15:00.
data_description.globird_zerocharge_import_cap_kwh: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom. This is the daily kWh cap that receives the ZeroCharge free-import credit.

globird_portal.title: GloBird portal
globird_portal.description: Connect your GloBird account to expose billing, usage, latest data, ZeroHero readiness, and cost summary sensors.

data_description.connect_globird_portal: When enabled, PowerSync fetches account and usage metrics from the GloBird portal. Pricing optimization continues to use the existing PowerSync tariff/AEMO model.

data_description.globird_email: The email address you use to log in to the GloBird portal.
data_description.globird_password: Your GloBird portal password. Stored securely in Home Assistant.

epex.title: EPEX day-ahead pricing
epex.description: Configure European day-ahead pricing. Prices are sourced from ENTSO-E via the EPEX Predictor API (free, no account needed).

data_description.epex_region: Your EPEX electricity market bidding zone. Most users need just their country code (DE, AT, BE, NL, FR, etc.).
data_description.epex_surcharge: Fixed network fees, levies, and other charges in ct/kWh. Added to the wholesale price before tax.
data_description.epex_tax_percent: VAT percentage applied on top of the total price. Example: 21 for Belgium, 19 for Germany.
data_description.epex_export_rate: Fixed feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. Set to 0 to use the wholesale price for export valuation.
data_description.epex_import_price_entity: Optional Home Assistant sensor for actual import costs. Use ct/kWh or EUR/kWh; a price_values attribute can provide a forecast.
data_description.epex_export_price_entity: Optional Home Assistant sensor for actual export earnings. Use ct/kWh or EUR/kWh; a price_values attribute can provide a forecast.

octopus.title: Octopus Energy tariff
octopus.description: Select your Octopus product and GSP region. Find your GSP region on your Octopus bill or at {octopus_url}.

data_description.octopus_product: Agile = half-hourly dynamic. Go/Intelligent Go = cheap overnight EV rates. Flux/Intelligent Flux = solar+battery optimised. Tracker = daily wholesale.
data_description.octopus_region: Your Grid Supply Point region (single letter A-P). Check your Octopus bill or account dashboard.

octopus_saving_sessions.title: Saving sessions
octopus_saving_sessions.description: PowerSync automatically exports your battery during Octopus Saving Sessions to earn Octopoints. Connect via Octopus API or the Bottlecap Dave integration.

data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_enabled: Enable automatic battery export during Octopus Saving Sessions.
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_source: Direct uses the Octopus GraphQL API (requires API key). Entity reads from Bottlecap Dave's Octopus integration.
data_description.octopus_api_key: Your Octopus API key from the developer dashboard (not the same as your public tariff API key).
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_entity: Entity ID of the Bottlecap Dave saving sessions event entity (e.g. event.octopus_energy_saving_sessions).
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_auto_join: Automatically join available saving sessions. Only works in direct mode.

data_description.skip_tariff: Check to use default estimation instead of configuring custom rates.
data_description.plan_name: Optional label for this tariff plan. Shown in the integration card.
data_description.tariff_type: Flat rate uses a single price all day. Time of Use lets you add multiple periods with different rates.
data_description.flat_rate: Only used for flat rate tariff type.
data_description.offpeak_rate: Rate for hours not covered by any period you define.
data_description.fit_rate: Default export earnings for off-peak hours. Use a negative value when you pay to export.

data_description.period_type: e.g. Peak, Shoulder, Off-Peak, Super Off-Peak.
data_description.period_days: Which days this period applies to (weekdays, weekends, or all).
data_description.import_rate: What you pay per kWh to import during this period.
data_description.export_rate: What you earn per kWh for exporting during this period. Use a negative value when you pay to export.
data_description.remove_period: Choose a period and continue to remove it. Leave this as Keep all added periods when adding a new period.
data_description.add_another: Uncheck when done. Remaining hours become off-peak.

nz_retailer.title: NZ electricity retailer
nz_retailer.description: Select your retailer and distribution zone. Rates will be pre-filled and can be adjusted in the next step.

data_description.nz_retailer: Your NZ electricity retailer. Rates are pre-filled from the selected plan.
data_description.nz_distribution_zone: Your local network distribution zone. Check your electricity bill if unsure.

nz_rates.title: NZ electricity rates
nz_rates.description: Adjust rates to match your actual plan. All values are in NZD cents per kWh.

data_description.nz_shoulder_rate: Weekday daytime and weekend daytime rate.
data_description.nz_daily_supply: Fixed daily supply charge from your retailer.

battery_system.title: Battery system
battery_system.description: Select the battery or hybrid inverter installed at your site. This determines which connection protocol PowerSync uses.

data_description.battery_system: If you have multiple battery brands, choose the primary system that PowerSync should control.

custom_battery.title: Custom external controller
custom_battery.description: Select the Home Assistant entities PowerSync should use to model your battery system. PowerSync will generate optimizer plans in monitoring mode and will not send control commands.

data_description.custom_battery_level_entity: State of charge as a percentage.
data_description.custom_battery_power_entity: Battery power. Positive means discharging; negative means charging.
data_description.custom_grid_power_entity: Grid power. Positive means importing; negative means exporting.
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will never plan discharge below this percentage.
data_description.optimization_battery_capacity_wh: Total usable battery capacity in kWh for the external battery system.
data_description.optimization_max_charge_w: Maximum charge power in kW that the planner may schedule.
data_description.optimization_max_discharge_w: Maximum battery/inverter discharge power in kW that the planner may schedule for loads and export.
data_description.optimization_max_grid_export_w: Site/DNSP export cap in kW. Leave blank to auto-detect where available; set 0 for zero-export sites. This does not limit battery power used by house loads.
data_description.optimization_max_grid_import_w: Maximum site grid import in kW. Set 0 for no site import cap.
data_description.optimization_allow_grid_charge: When disabled, the planner will not schedule forced grid charging.

optimization_provider.title: Optimization mode
optimization_provider.description: Choose how PowerSync schedules your battery. The built-in option uses your {battery_name}'s native TOU scheduler; Smart Optimization runs a cost-minimising LP solver inside PowerSync.

data_description.optimization_provider: Smart Optimization generally delivers lower bills but requires the HiGHS solver (highspy). The native mode is simpler and uses your battery's own scheduling logic.

ml_options.title: Smart Optimization settings
ml_options.description: Configure PowerSync's Smart Optimization limits. Advanced optimizer controls change how the solver evaluates grid charging, import/export limits, spreading, idle handling, and forecast reserve.

data_description.optimization_provider: Smart Optimization uses an LP solver to minimise costs. Native mode uses your battery's built-in TOU scheduler.
data_description.optimization_enabled: Turn the LP optimizer on or off without changing the saved Smart Optimization settings.
data_description.optimization_auto_apply_reserve: Preserves the selected minimum as a forecast buffer by adding expected home load between an export window and the next charge. Natural self-consumption may continue toward that buffer; Auto-Apply never lowers below your selected minimum or changes the hardware backup reserve.
data_description.optimization_ev_integration: Adds EV demand from PowerSync-managed charger schedules to Smart Optimization. Leave this off when the planned EV load forecast sensor already represents the same charging demand.
data_description.optimization_load_entity: Optional recorder-backed live household load sensor for Smart Optimization history. Use a no-EV load sensor here if your home load history includes EV charging and you also add planned EV load separately.
data_description.optimization_planned_ev_load_entity: Optional sensor with a planned_load attribute for forecast-only EV demand. PowerSync always adds this sensor to the load forecast when selected, but does not control the charger.
data_description.monitoring_mode: Block battery and inverter control commands while still updating prices, sensors, and plans for observation.
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will not intentionally export battery energy below this percentage. Natural self-consumption may continue to the separate hardware backup reserve. Default 20% is a good starting point.
data_description.hardware_backup_reserve: The battery's own backup reserve for grid outages. PowerSync restores this level after temporary hold or force-control modes.
data_description.optimization_battery_capacity_wh: Total usable battery capacity in kWh. This should match the battery specs shown in the mobile app.
data_description.optimization_max_charge_w: Maximum grid charge power in kW that the LP optimizer may request.
data_description.optimization_max_discharge_w: Maximum battery/inverter discharge power in kW that the LP optimizer may request for loads and export.
data_description.optimization_max_grid_export_w: Site/DNSP export cap in kW. Leave blank to auto-detect where available; set 0 for zero-export sites. This does not limit battery power used by house loads.
data_description.optimization_max_grid_import_w: Maximum total site grid import in kW. Set 0 for no site import cap; solar can still add charging power above this limit.
data_description.optimization_max_grid_charge_price: Advanced: never plan forced battery charging from grid import above this import price. Set 0 to disable the price cap.
data_description.optimization_grid_charge_soc_cap: Advanced: stop forced grid charging once forecast battery SOC reaches this cap. Solar surplus can still charge above the cap.
data_description.optimization_allow_grid_charge: When disabled, Smart Optimization can still charge from solar surplus but will not plan forced battery charging from grid import.
data_description.optimization_spread_export_enabled: When enabled on supported batteries, Smart Optimization spreads planned battery export across the full eligible export window instead of using maximum discharge power.
data_description.optimization_spread_import_enabled: When enabled on supported batteries, Smart Optimization spreads planned grid charging across each same-price import window instead of using maximum charge power.
data_description.optimization_disable_idle: Replace ordinary Smart Optimization idle hold actions with self-consumption for any electricity provider. Holds required to meet an active Charge By Time target are preserved.
data_description.profit_max_enabled: Let Smart Optimization prioritise profitable export opportunities instead of holding battery charge for later by default.
data_description.charge_by_time_enabled: Force Smart Optimization to reach the configured target SOC by the configured time.
data_description.charge_by_time_target_time: HH:MM time for Charge By Time to reach the configured target SOC. Default 17:15.
data_description.charge_by_time_target_soc: Battery SOC Charge By Time should target by the configured time. Default 100%.

sungrow.title: Sungrow Modbus connection
sungrow.description: Connect to your Sungrow SH-series hybrid inverter via Modbus TCP.

data_description.sungrow_host: Local IP of your Sungrow inverter or WiNet-S dongle (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
data_description.sungrow_port: Default Modbus TCP port is 502. Only change if customised on the inverter.
data_description.sungrow_slave_id: Default is 1. Only change if you have multiple devices on the same bus.

foxess_connection.title: FoxESS connection type
foxess_connection.description: Choose how to connect to your FoxESS inverter.

data_description.foxess_connection_type: Modbus TCP connects over LAN/WiFi. RS485 Serial provides direct register access. FoxESS Cloud API uses Open API control with cloud-rate-limit latency. Entity bridge reuses an existing foxess_modbus integration instead of opening another Modbus connection.

foxess_tcp.title: FoxESS Modbus TCP
foxess_tcp.description: Connect to your FoxESS inverter over the local network. The model family will be auto-detected.

data_description.foxess_host: Local IP of your FoxESS inverter. H3 Smart may appear as 'espressif' on your network.

foxess_serial.title: FoxESS RS485 serial
foxess_serial.description: Connect to your FoxESS inverter via RS485 serial for full register access including BMS data.

data_description.foxess_serial_baudrate: Default 9600. Only change if the inverter is configured differently.

foxess_model.title: Confirm FoxESS model
foxess_model.description: Connection successful. Please confirm your inverter model.

data_description.foxess_model_family: H3-Pro and H3 Smart use the same register set. The H3 Smart has built-in WiFi Modbus.

foxess_cloud.title: FoxESS Cloud
foxess_cloud.description: Connect FoxESS Cloud for Open API control and schedule sync. Required for Cloud API-only setup; optional for Modbus setup.

data_description.foxess_cloud_api_key: Get your API key from foxesscloud.com > User Centre > API Management.
data_description.foxess_cloud_device_sn: Inverter serial number from FoxESS Cloud. Auto-selected when the account has one device.

foxess_entity.title: FoxESS entity bridge
foxess_entity.description: Bridge through Nathan Marlor's foxess_modbus entities. PowerSync reads sensors and sends supported controls through Home Assistant services.

data_description.foxess_entity_config_entry_id: Select the foxess_modbus integration entry PowerSync should read and control. If there is only one entry, PowerSync selects it automatically.
data_description.foxess_entity_prefix: Optional fallback for renamed entities. Example: foxess for sensor.foxess_battery_soc and select.foxess_work_mode.

goodwe_connection.title: GoodWe inverter connection
goodwe_connection.description: Connect to your GoodWe hybrid inverter. The model will be auto-detected.

data_description.goodwe_host: Local IP address of your GoodWe inverter.
data_description.goodwe_protocol: UDP uses direct GoodWe control on port 8899. TCP uses Modbus on port 502 for LAN/Kit-20 setups.
data_description.goodwe_ems_control_mode: Choose how PowerSync sends GoodWe EMS commands. Direct IP requires UDP 8899 control. For TCP / LAN Kit-20 setups, PowerSync auto-uses detected GoodWe Experimental EMS entities because direct operation-mode writes do not reliably control the inverter. TCP / LAN Kit-20 telemetry can also use detected GoodWe HA sensors to avoid a second direct TCP/502 polling client.
data_description.goodwe_ems_entity_prefix: Required only when EMS control method is Home Assistant entity control. PowerSync will auto-detect a matching EMS entity pair when possible. You can also enter only the prefix for the GoodWe Experimental EMS controls. Example: goodwe uses select.goodwe_ems_mode and number.goodwe_ems_power_limit.

esy_sunhome.title: ESY Sunhome connection
esy_sunhome.description: PowerSync bridges through the ESY Sunhome integration. Select the ESY Sunhome config entry to use.

data_description.esy_config_entry_id: The ESY Sunhome integration entry to read sensors and send mode commands through. Install it via HACS if it does not appear here.

saj_h2_battery.title: SAJ H2 connection
saj_h2_battery.description: PowerSync bridges through the SAJ H2 Modbus integration. Install it from HACS (search 'SAJ H2 Inverter Modbus') and configure your inverter, then return here.

data_description.saj_config_entry_id: The saj_h2_modbus integration entry to read sensors and send passive charge/discharge commands through.
data_description.saj_battery_capacity_kwh: Usable battery capacity in kWh. Used by the LP optimizer.
data_description.saj_inverter_rated_kw: Inverter AC rated power in kW. Read off the model number — e.g. HS2-10K-T2-5 → 10. Used to convert the LP's requested watts into the SAJ register encoding (% × 10 of rated).

fronius_reserva_battery.title: Fronius GEN24 storage connection
fronius_reserva_battery.description: PowerSync bridges through the Fronius Modbus integration. Install it from HACS and configure your GEN24 BYD or Reserva storage system, then return here.

data_description.fronius_reserva_config_entry_id: The fronius_modbus integration entry to read sensors and send GEN24 storage-control commands through.
data_description.fronius_reserva_battery_capacity_kwh: Usable battery capacity in kWh. Used by the LP optimizer.
data_description.fronius_reserva_max_charge_kw: Maximum grid charge power in kW. Used by dispatch commands and the LP optimizer.
data_description.fronius_reserva_max_discharge_kw: Maximum grid discharge/export power in kW. Used by dispatch commands and the LP optimizer.

neovolt_battery.title: Neovolt connection
neovolt_battery.description: PowerSync bridges through the Neovolt Modbus integration. Install it from HACS and configure your Neovolt/Bytewatt system, then return here.

data_description.neovolt_config_entry_id: The Neovolt integration entry to read combined sensors and send dispatch commands through.
data_description.neovolt_config_entry_ids: Select every Neovolt inverter entry PowerSync should read and control.
data_description.neovolt_max_charge_kw: Maximum charge power in kW. Used by the LP optimizer and dispatch command limits.
data_description.neovolt_max_discharge_kw: Maximum discharge power in kW. Used by the LP optimizer and dispatch command limits.
data_description.neovolt_battery_capacities_kwh: Optional comma-separated usable battery capacities in kWh. With multiple Neovolt integrations, values follow the selected integration order. With one integration, multiple values are summed. Example: 20.1, 30.2. Leave blank to use Neovolt's capacity sensors.
data_description.neovolt_surplus_balancer_mode: Controls independent NeoVolt stack balancing. Auto balances multiple selected Neovolt integrations whenever PowerSync is running; the Smart Optimization switch does not control it. Disable this if your batteries already have a parallel/BMS coordinator.
data_description.neovolt_soc_balance_tolerance: PowerSync will not force-charge a higher-SOC parked stack while another stack is behind by more than this percentage.

anker_solix.title: Anker Solix connection
anker_solix.description: Choose direct X1 Modbus, Anker's official local HA integration, or the unofficial cloud HA bridge. Direct Modbus and the official local bridge are preferred for dispatch control.

data_description.anker_solix_connection_type: Direct X1 Modbus uses local TCP port 502. Official HA bridges through anker_solix_official. Cloud HA bridges through the unofficial anker_solix integration and may be telemetry-only.
data_description.anker_solix_modbus_host: Local IP address of the Anker SOLIX X1 system. Third-party control and Modbus TCP must be enabled in Anker settings.
data_description.anker_solix_config_entry_id: Optional. Select the Anker HA integration entry PowerSync should read from when more than one is configured.
data_description.anker_solix_entity_prefix: Optional. Used when Anker entities have a shared prefix and PowerSync needs help matching the right device.
data_description.anker_solix_battery_capacity_kwh: Usable battery capacity in kWh. Used by the LP optimizer when the Anker path does not expose rated energy.
data_description.anker_solix_max_charge_kw: Maximum charge power in kW. Used by dispatch commands and the LP optimizer.
data_description.anker_solix_max_discharge_kw: Maximum discharge/export power in kW. Used by dispatch commands and the LP optimizer.

sigenergy_credentials.title: Sigenergy Cloud login
sigenergy_credentials.description: Enter your Sigenergy Cloud API credentials to connect your system.

data_description.sigenergy_username: Your Sigenergy Cloud account email address.
data_description.sigenergy_device_id: Optional 13-digit userDeviceId. Leave blank for modern accounts, including EU accounts that only show user_id, stationId, or stationCode.
data_description.sigenergy_cloud_region: Select the Sigen Cloud data centre for your account. Europe uses the EU endpoint.
data_description.sigenergy_pass_enc: Pre-encoded password from the Sigenergy API. Leave blank unless you know what this is.

sigenergy_station.title: Sigenergy station
sigenergy_station.description: Select your Sigenergy station. If the list is unavailable, enter the ID manually.

data_description.sigenergy_station_id: To find your Station ID, ask SigenAI 'Tell me my StationID' in the Sigenergy app.

sigenergy_modbus.title: Sigenergy Modbus connection
sigenergy_modbus.description: Configure Modbus TCP to read real-time solar, battery, and grid power data from your Sigenergy system.

data_description.sigenergy_modbus_host: Local IP address of your Sigenergy inverter or gateway (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
data_description.sigenergy_modbus_port: Default Modbus TCP port is 502. Only change if customised.
data_description.sigenergy_modbus_slave_id: Default is 247. PowerSync auto-detects the correct registers.

sigenergy_dc_curtailment.title: DC solar curtailment
sigenergy_dc_curtailment.description: When enabled, PowerSync limits DC solar production when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh via Modbus. For AC-coupled inverters, configure curtailment separately.

data_description.sigenergy_dc_curtailment_enabled: Reduce DC solar output when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh. Requires Modbus write access to the Sigenergy system.
data_description.sigenergy_export_limit_kw: Your DNSP grid export limit in kW. Leave blank to auto-detect from inverter registers. Prevents PowerSync from ever exceeding your grid connection allowance.

tesla_provider.title: Tesla API connection
tesla_provider.description:
{fleet_detected}

Choose how PowerSync connects to your Tesla account for Powerwall control, and optionally for vehicle commands.

data_description.tesla_api_provider: Controls how PowerSync reads and writes your Powerwall TOU schedule. PowerSync.cc is free and handles OAuth for you.
data_description.tesla_ev_api_provider: Only needed if you want PowerSync to send commands to a Tesla vehicle (e.g. start/stop charging). Choose None if you only have a Powerwall or use BLE.

tesla_ev_teslemetry_token.title: Teslemetry vehicle token
tesla_ev_teslemetry_token.description: The Teslemetry HA integration is not installed, so PowerSync needs a token to send vehicle commands directly.

data_description.tesla_ev_teslemetry_token: Get a token from [{teslemetry_url}]({teslemetry_url}). This is only used for vehicle commands -- your Powerwall uses a separate connection.

teslemetry.title: Teslemetry API key
teslemetry.description: Enter your Teslemetry API token to connect PowerSync to your Tesla account via the Teslemetry proxy (~$4/month).

data_description.teslemetry_api_token: Get a token from teslemetry.com. Paste the full token string.

powersync.title: Sign in with PowerSync
powersync.description: Sign in with your Tesla account at the link below. You will receive a token starting with `psync_` -- paste it here.

data_description.teslemetry_api_token: Open {auth_url} in a browser, sign in with Tesla, and copy the psync_ token. Your Tesla credentials are never shared with PowerSync.

powersync_reauth.title: Re-authenticate with PowerSync
powersync_reauth.description: Your PowerSync token is no longer valid. Sign in again at **{auth_url}** and paste the new `psync_` token below.

teslemetry_reauth.title: Re-authenticate Teslemetry
teslemetry_reauth.description: Your Teslemetry token is no longer valid. Get a new one from teslemetry.com and paste it below.

site_selection.title: Select your sites
site_selection.description: Match your Tesla energy site with your electricity account. If you only have one of each, they are pre-selected.

data_description.tesla_energy_site_id: The Powerwall or Solar site that PowerSync will control. If you have multiple Tesla sites, choose the one at this address.
data_description.powerwall_local_ip: Optional. Provides direct LAN access for live local polling and fast gateway writes — pairing handles authentication via RSA signing, no password required.
data_description.amber_site_id: If you have multiple Amber NMIs, select the one associated with this Powerwall.
data_description.auto_sync_enabled: When enabled, PowerSync uploads a new TOU tariff schedule to your battery every 5 minutes based on current Amber prices.
data_description.amber_forecast_type: Predicted is balanced. Low assumes cheaper prices (more aggressive charging). High assumes expensive prices (more conservative).
data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery from exporting to grid when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh. This is the main toggle -- must be enabled for any curtailment to work.

weather_setup.title: Weather & solar forecast
weather_setup.description: Configure weather automations and solar forecasting. If Solcast HA integration is already installed, PowerSync detects it automatically.

data_description.weather_location: Suburb, city, postcode, or lat,lon coordinates (e.g. 'Melbourne', '3000', '-37.81,144.96'). Leave blank to auto-detect from timezone.
data_description.openweathermap_api_key: Free API key from openweathermap.org for weather-based automations (sunny, cloudy, rain, snow, and storm triggers). Leave blank to disable.
data_description.weather_entity: Optional. Used for temperature-aware load forecasting if Home Assistant has a weather entity. Leave blank when using OpenWeatherMap.
data_description.solar_forecast_provider: Choose which solar forecast provider PowerSync tries first. If the selected provider has no usable forecast, PowerSync falls back to the other supported provider.
data_description.solcast_enabled: Enable solar production forecasting. If the Solcast or Open-Meteo Solar Forecast HA integration is installed, PowerSync reads from its sensors automatically (no API calls needed).
data_description.solcast_api_key: Only needed if you do NOT have the Solcast HA integration. Get a free key from toolkit.solcast.com.au (10 calls/day on hobbyist tier).
data_description.solcast_resource_id: Only needed without the Solcast HA integration. For split arrays, enter comma-separated IDs (e.g. abc123,def456).
data_description.solcast_estimate_type: Choose which Solcast production estimate PowerSync uses for sensors and optimisation.

demand_charges.title: Demand charge tracking
demand_charges.description: Track peak demand during capacity-based billing periods. Leave unchecked to skip if your plan has no demand charges.

data.demand_allow_grid_charging: Allow grid charging during demand windows

data_description.demand_charge_enabled: Track your peak grid draw during demand charge periods and calculate monthly capacity fees.
data_description.demand_charge_rate: Your network demand charge rate in dollars per kW of peak demand. Check your bill for this value.
data_description.demand_charge_start_time: Start time of the demand charge measurement period (24-hour format).
data_description.demand_charge_end_time: End time of the demand charge measurement period (24-hour format).
data_description.demand_allow_grid_charging: Allow battery grid charging during demand peak periods. Disable to protect against demand charge spikes.

curtailment_setup.title: Export curtailment
curtailment_setup.description: Configure automatic curtailment when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh. These settings stop battery export and optionally shut down solar.

data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery from exporting to grid when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh. This is the main toggle -- must be enabled for any curtailment to work.
data_description.ac_inverter_curtailment_enabled: Shut down a separate AC-coupled or string solar inverter (Fronius, Sungrow, GoodWe, Enphase) when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh. Do not enable this for the hybrid battery inverter already configured as the battery system.

inverter_brand_setup.title: AC-coupled inverter brand
inverter_brand_setup.description: Select your solar inverter brand for direct curtailment control when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh.

data_description.inverter_brand: The brand of your AC-coupled solar inverter. PowerSync uses brand-specific protocols for shutdown commands.

inverter_config_setup.title: {brand} inverter configuration
inverter_config_setup.description: Configure the connection to your {brand} inverter for curtailment control.

data_description.inverter_host: Local IP address of your inverter or gateway.
data_description.inverter_port: 502 for Modbus (Sungrow, Fronius, GoodWe, Huawei), 443 for Enphase.
data_description.inverter_token: Optional if using Enlighten credentials. Manual tokens expire in 12 hours for installer accounts.
data_description.enphase_username: Your Enlighten account email for automatic JWT token refresh (recommended).
data_description.enphase_serial: Leave blank to auto-detect from IQ Gateway.
data_description.enphase_normal_profile: Full name of your normal grid profile. Example: 'AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 Australia A'.
data_description.enphase_zero_export_profile: Full name of a zero-export grid profile if available (for fallback curtailment).
data_description.enphase_is_installer: Enable if using an installer Enlighten account. Required for grid profile switching.
data_description.inverter_rated_power_w: SolarEdge rated AC output in watts. Used to convert load-following curtailment into the inverter's 0-100% active power limit.
data_description.inverter_restore_soc: Restore inverter when battery drops below this %. Ensures battery stays topped up before evening peak. Default: 98%.
data_description.fronius_load_following: Enable if you do NOT have a 0W export limit. Uses calculated power limits based on home load. Leave off if you have a 0W export profile (most users).

battery_management_mode.title: Battery management mode
battery_management_mode.description: Choose how PowerSync manages your battery.

data_description.battery_management_mode: Manual = PowerSync automations control charge/discharge. TOU Sync = prices synced to battery's built-in scheduler. Smart Optimization = LP solver minimises costs.
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: Intentional battery export stops at this level. Natural self-consumption may continue to the separate hardware backup reserve.

solax_battery.title: Solax Hybrid connection
solax_battery.description: PowerSync bridges through the homeassistant-solax-modbus integration. Install it from HACS (search 'Solax Modbus') and configure your inverter, then return here.

data_description.solax_config_entry_id: The solax_modbus integration entry to read sensors and send charge/discharge commands through.
data_description.solax_battery_capacity_kwh: Usable battery capacity in kWh. Used by the LP optimizer.
data_description.solax_battery_nominal_v: Nominal battery voltage in V (51.2 for LFP T-BAT, 102.4 for high-voltage packs).
data_description.solax_max_charge_current_a: Maximum charge current your battery supports (A).
data_description.solax_max_discharge_current_a: Maximum discharge current your battery supports (A).
data_description.solax_entity_prefix: Optional. Use when Solax Modbus creates more than one naming group. For entities like select.solax_modbus_charger_use_mode, enter solax_modbus.

alphaess_modbus.title: AlphaESS connection type
alphaess_modbus.description: Choose full local Modbus control or cloud-only monitoring. Cloud-only mode cannot send battery or inverter commands.

alphaess_cloud.title: AlphaESS Cloud API
alphaess_cloud.description: Cloud credentials are required for cloud-only monitoring and optional for Modbus telemetry fallback.

solaredge.title: SolarEdge battery and curtailment
solaredge.description: Connect to your SolarEdge system for Home Battery telemetry, battery dispatch, and active-power curtailment. Battery telemetry and dispatch are bridged through SolarEdge HA entities; curtailment uses Modbus TCP or entity fallback. Enable Modbus TCP and inverter-side power control in SetApp or the LCD menu first. Some firmware or grid profiles may reject third-party power-control writes.

data_description.solaredge_host: Local IP of the SolarEdge inverter or Modbus leader. Leave blank only when using HA entity fallback.
data_description.solaredge_slave_id: Default is 1. Only change if the inverter uses another Modbus unit ID.
data_description.solaredge_rated_power_w: Rated AC output in watts. Used to convert load-following requests into SolarEdge's 0-100% active power limit.
data_description.solaredge_entity_prefix: Optional. Used to match SolarEdge HA telemetry, storage-control, and active-power-limit entities when more than one naming group exists.
data_description.solaredge_dc_curtailment_enabled: When enabled, PowerSync sets SolarEdge active power to 0% during uneconomic export periods and restores it to 100% when export recovers.

solaredge_connection.title: SolarEdge battery and curtailment
solaredge_connection.description: Configure SolarEdge Home Battery telemetry, battery dispatch, and active-power curtailment. Dispatch uses writable SolarEdge HA storage-control entities; Modbus TCP and inverter-side power control must be enabled on the inverter for curtailment.

data_description.solaredge_host: Local IP of the SolarEdge inverter or Modbus leader. Leave blank only when using HA entity fallback.
data_description.solaredge_slave_id: Default is 1. Only change if the inverter uses another Modbus unit ID.
data_description.solaredge_rated_power_w: Rated AC output in watts. Used to convert load-following requests into SolarEdge's 0-100% active power limit.
data_description.solaredge_entity_prefix: Optional. Used when direct Modbus is unavailable and a SolarEdge Modbus HA integration exposes entities such as number.solaredge_active_power_limit.
data_description.solaredge_dc_curtailment_enabled: When enabled, PowerSync sets SolarEdge active power to 0% during uneconomic export periods and restores it to 100% when export recovers.

error.invalid_auth: Token rejected by the API -- check you copied the full token string with no extra spaces.
error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the API server. Check your internet connection and that Home Assistant has outbound HTTPS access.
error.invalid_globird_auth: Email or password is incorrect. Check your GloBird portal login credentials.
error.captcha_required: GloBird requested captcha verification. Sign in through the GloBird portal in a browser, then try again.
error.no_sites: No Amber sites found for this token. Verify the token belongs to an account with an active Amber site.
error.no_energy_sites: No Tesla energy sites found. Ensure your Powerwall or Solar system is set up in the Tesla app before continuing.
error.no_token_provided: Paste a token in the field above. See the hint text for where to find it.
error.tesla_fleet_not_installed: Tesla Fleet integration is not installed in Home Assistant. Add it from Settings > Devices & Services > Add Integration > Tesla Fleet, then return here.
error.powerwall_gateway_invalid: Enter a bare Powerwall IP/hostname or an HTTP(S) URL without credentials, a path, query, or invalid port.
error.aemo_region_required: Select your NEM region when spike detection is enabled. Check your electricity bill for your NEM zone.
error.aemo_required_in_aemo_mode: Spike detection is recommended for Globird and VPP plans with spike export payments. Disable only if your plan does not include this feature.
error.missing_credentials: Email and password are both required. Enter your Sigenergy account credentials.
error.invalid_device_id: Device ID must be exactly 13 digits. Leave blank if your account does not require one.
error.no_stations: No Sigenergy stations found. Check that your Sigenergy account has at least one commissioned station.
error.no_station_selected: Select a Sigenergy station from the list to continue.
error.modbus_host_required: IP address is required -- enter the local IP of your Sigenergy inverter or gateway.
error.sungrow_host_required: IP address is required -- enter the local IP of your Sungrow inverter or WiNet-S dongle.
error.modbus_conflict: Connected but received invalid data (SOC > 100%). Another integration is likely using Modbus on the same port. Disable other Modbus integrations for this inverter and retry.
error.sungrow_modbus_conflict: The AC inverter curtailment path cannot use the same Sungrow Modbus IP, port, and slave ID as the battery system. Disable this option for a hybrid-only setup, or configure a separate AC-coupled/string inverter endpoint.
error.alphaess_cloud_required: AlphaESS Cloud monitoring requires both an App ID and App Secret.
error.goodwe_entity_prefix_required: Enter the GoodWe Experimental entity prefix, for example goodwe.
error.goodwe_entity_unavailable: PowerSync could not validate the GoodWe PV power, grid export limit, and grid export limit switch entities for that prefix.
error.foxess_host_required: IP address is required -- enter the local IP of your FoxESS inverter.
error.foxess_tcp_failed: Cannot connect via Modbus TCP. Check: (1) IP is correct (H3 Smart shows as 'espressif'), (2) firmware is current, (3) port 502 is reachable from Home Assistant.
error.foxess_serial_required: Serial port path is required -- e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 for a USB-to-RS485 adapter.
error.foxess_cloud_auth_failed: FoxESS Cloud rejected the API key. Verify the key is correct and that API access is enabled on your FoxESS account.
error.foxess_cloud_required: FoxESS Cloud API key is required for Cloud API-only setup.
error.foxess_cloud_device_required: Select the FoxESS inverter serial number to use for Cloud API-only setup.
error.foxess_cloud_connection_error: Cannot reach FoxESS Cloud. Check your internet connection and retry.
error.foxess_entity_required: Select a foxess_modbus integration entry, or enter an entity prefix such as foxess.
error.foxess_entity_missing_entities: PowerSync could not find the required foxess_modbus entities. Required entities include battery SOC, grid power or import/export sensors, work mode, force charge/discharge power, and minimum SOC.
error.foxess_entity_connect_failed: Cannot validate the FoxESS entity bridge. Check that the foxess_modbus integration is loaded and its entities are available.
error.goodwe_connect_failed: Cannot connect to GoodWe inverter. Verify the IP address is correct and the inverter is powered on and reachable from Home Assistant.
error.goodwe_no_battery: This GoodWe model (DT/MS/XS series) does not support battery control. Only ET/EH/ES/EM series hybrid inverters are supported.
error.goodwe_ems_prefix_required: Enter the GoodWe EMS entity prefix, or change EMS control method to Direct IP control.
error.goodwe_ems_entities_missing: Could not find the GoodWe HA EMS entities for that prefix. Check that the GoodWe Experimental integration exposes matching EMS mode and EMS power limit entities such as select.goodwe_ems_mode and number.goodwe_ems_power_limit, then enter only the prefix.
error.esy_sunhome_not_installed: The ESY Sunhome integration is not installed. Install it via HACS (search 'ESY Sunhome') and add a device before continuing.
error.esy_sunhome_no_device: No ESY Sunhome device found in the selected integration entry. Verify the ESY Sunhome integration is set up and has a paired device.
error.invalid_api_key: Flow Power rejected that API key. Copy the current value from More > Web Data Access and try again.
error.invalid_site: Select a Flow Power site from the list.
error.zaptec_auth_failed: Zaptec Cloud login failed. Check that your email and password are correct.
error.zaptec_missing_credentials: Both email and password are required for Zaptec Cloud authentication.
error.no_prices: No prices found for this tariff and region combination. Verify your Octopus product and GSP region match your account.
error.optimizer_not_installed: Smart Optimization requires the HiGHS solver (highspy), which is not installed. Check Home Assistant logs for installation errors.
error.invalid_tariff: This tariff code is not recognised for the selected network distributor. Check the code on your electricity bill.
error.invalid_token_format: Token format is not recognised. PowerSync tokens start with 'psync_' -- make sure you copied the full string.
error.invalid_time: Enter a valid 24-hour time in HH:MM format.
error.cloud_flow_grid_entity_required: Select a grid power sensor -- it is required to enable the PowerSync Cloud energy-flow reporter.
error.invalid_postcode: Enter a valid four-digit Australian postcode.
error.covau_no_supported_plans: No current fixture-backed CovaU SolarMax plan is available for that postcode.
error.covau_unsupported_plan: Select one of the supported SolarMax plan IDs or use the validated manual fallback.
error.covau_distributor_mismatch: The distributor must match the selected AER SolarMax plan.
error.covau_energy_meter_invalid: Select a monotonic total_increasing energy sensor using Wh, kWh, or MWh.

Make sure you selected the correct Solax Modbus integration. If your entities use a prefix like solax_modbus, enter that in Entity prefix override.
error.solax_connect_failed: Failed to validate Solax entities. Check the selected integration entry and try again.
error.saj_missing_entities: Could not find the required SAJ H2 entities in the selected saj_h2_modbus integration entry. Make sure the integration is installed, the inverter is online, and its entities have been created in Home Assistant.
error.saj_connect_failed: Failed to validate SAJ H2 entities. Check the selected integration entry and try again.
error.fronius_reserva_missing_entities: Could not find the required Fronius GEN24 storage entities in the selected fronius_modbus integration entry. The first missing entity was: {first_missing}. Make sure the integration is installed, the system is online, and storage entities have been created in Home Assistant.
error.fronius_reserva_connect_failed: Failed to validate Fronius GEN24 storage entities. Check the selected integration entry and try again.
error.neovolt_missing_entities: Could not find the required Neovolt entities in the selected Neovolt integration entry. Make sure the integration is installed, the system is online, and its entities have been created in Home Assistant.
error.neovolt_connect_failed: Failed to validate Neovolt entities. Check the selected integration entry and try again.
error.neovolt_capacity_invalid: Enter positive battery capacities in kWh, separated by commas.
error.anker_solix_host_required: Enter the local IP address of the Anker SOLIX X1 system.
error.anker_solix_ha_not_installed: The selected Anker HA integration is not installed or has no configured entries. Install and configure it before selecting this connection type.
error.anker_solix_connect_failed: Could not validate the Anker Solix connection. For direct Modbus, check IP, port 502, slave ID, and third-party control. For HA bridge mode, check that the Anker integration exposes SOC and solar power entities.
error.solaredge_host_required: Enter the SolarEdge inverter IP address, or enter an HA entity prefix for entity fallback.
error.solaredge_connect_failed: Could not connect to SolarEdge via Modbus or find a matching SolarEdge active power limit entity. Check Modbus TCP, power-control settings, firmware/grid profile support, and the entity prefix.
error.solaredge_rated_power_required: Enter the SolarEdge inverter rated AC power in watts.

abort.already_configured: PowerSync is already configured. To change settings, use the Configure button on the integration card.
abort.no_energy_sites: No Tesla energy sites found. Ensure you have a Powerwall or Solar system set up in the Tesla app before adding PowerSync.
abort.no_stations: No Sigenergy stations found. Check that your credentials are correct and your station is commissioned.

error.goodwe_entity_unavailable: PowerSync could not validate the GoodWe PV power, grid export limit, and grid export limit switch entities for that prefix.

error.covau_unsupported_plan: Select one of the supported SolarMax plan IDs or keep the currently cached manual plan.
error.covau_distributor_mismatch: The distributor must match the selected AER SolarMax plan.
error.covau_energy_meter_invalid: Select a monotonic total_increasing energy sensor using Wh, kWh, or MWh.
error.confirm_required: Confirm the history relink before applying changes.
error.no_ready_history_relinks: No ready history relinks were found. Check the preview summary.
error.network_export_invalid_mode: Select a valid network export mode.
error.powerwall_gateway_invalid: Enter a bare Powerwall IP/hostname or an HTTP(S) URL without credentials, a path, query, or invalid port.

error.network_export_der_attestation_required: Active mode requires confirmation that every exporting inverter and battery is covered by the approved site-wide limiter.
error.network_export_aggregate_required: Multi-phase active mode requires one aggregate connection-point limit. Per-phase sources are monitoring-only.
error.network_export_source_unregistered: The limit source must be owned by a registered Home Assistant integration.
error.network_export_source_untrusted: Templates and PowerSync-owned entities cannot arm active export. Select the original certified vendor or inverter entity.

init.title: PowerSync settings
init.description: Settings are grouped for your selected battery, provider, and EV setup. Optional specialist features are under Advanced settings.

advanced.title: Advanced settings
advanced.description: Optional features for specialist tariffs, export controls, extra inverters, forecasting, and maintenance. You can safely leave these alone unless they apply to your setup.

battery_system.title: Battery / control method
battery_system.description: Change the battery or hybrid inverter that PowerSync controls. The selected method is saved before the connection page opens, and the integration reloads after the connection settings are saved.

data_description.battery_system: Choose the primary battery, inverter, or external controller PowerSync should use.

custom_battery.title: Custom external controller
custom_battery.description: Select the Home Assistant entities PowerSync should use to model your battery system. PowerSync will generate optimizer plans in monitoring mode and will not send control commands.

alphaess_connection.title: AlphaESS connection
alphaess_connection.description: Update AlphaESS Modbus settings and optional AlphaESS Cloud fallback credentials.

anker_solix.title: Anker Solix connection
anker_solix.description: Update Anker Solix direct Modbus or HA integration bridge settings.

history_relink.title: Sungrow history relink
history_relink.description:
Relink PowerSync Sungrow energy entities to matching mkaiser Sungrow entity IDs so the Energy Dashboard can keep using existing long-term statistics. Create a Home Assistant backup before applying.

{summary}

data_description.confirm: Only mappings marked ready will be applied. Ambiguous, missing, and blocked mappings are skipped.

auto_update.title: PowerSync auto-update
auto_update.description: Install available HACS updates for PowerSync at the scheduled time, then restart Home Assistant.

data_description.auto_update_enabled: When enabled, PowerSync looks for an install-capable HACS update entity once per day.
data_description.auto_update_time: Local Home Assistant time. Default is 03:00.

cloud_flow.title: PowerSync Cloud energy-flow reporter
cloud_flow.description: Push your live grid, solar, battery, and load power to PowerSync Cloud so it can drive charge-on-solar decisions even without a Tesla energy site. Sign convention: grid power should read positive when importing from the grid and negative when exporting (enable 'Invert grid sign' if your sensor reads the opposite way); battery power should read positive when discharging and negative when charging. Requires the PowerSync Cloud flow-reporter beta flag on your account -- pushes are simply skipped until it's granted.

data.cloud_flow_report: Report energy flow to PowerSync Cloud

data_description.cloud_flow_report: When enabled, PowerSync pushes a flow update to PowerSync Cloud roughly every 30 seconds.
data_description.cloud_flow_grid_entity: Required to enable reporting. Positive = importing from the grid, negative = exporting.
data_description.cloud_flow_battery_soc_entity: Optional. Battery level as a percentage (0-100%).
data_description.cloud_flow_invert_grid: Enable if your grid power sensor reads positive on export instead of import.

pricing.title: Electricity pricing
pricing.description: Change your electricity provider or update pricing settings. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

data_description.electricity_provider: If your retailer is not listed, choose Other / Custom TOU to enter rates manually.

agl_options.title: AGL Battery Rewards
agl_options.description: Update the total AGL feed-in rates from your current offer. PowerSync applies the evening rate every day from 17:00 to 21:00, then opens your address-specific import tariff for review.

data_description.agl_battery_rewards_peak_export_rate: Total export rate, not a bonus added to the off-peak rate. AGL currently advertises 28c/kWh; confirm your offer.
data_description.agl_battery_rewards_offpeak_export_rate: Export rate outside 17:00-21:00. AGL currently advertises 3c/kWh; confirm your offer.

covau_options.title: CovaU SolarMax pricing
covau_options.description: Confirm the exact AER plan snapshot, distributor, and authoritative connection-point settlement meters. Enable refresh to explicitly compare and cache a new snapshot for the same plan ID; PowerSync never selects a successor automatically.

tesla_connection.title: Tesla API connection
tesla_connection.description: Update your Powerwall and vehicle API providers. Changes take effect immediately.

data.update_teslemetry_token: New API token (leave blank to keep current)

data_description.tesla_api_provider: Controls how PowerSync reads and writes your Powerwall TOU schedule. PowerSync.cc is free and handles OAuth for you.
data_description.tesla_ev_api_provider: Only needed if you want PowerSync to send commands to a Tesla vehicle (e.g. start/stop charging). Choose None if you only have a Powerwall or use BLE.
data_description.powerwall_local_ip: Optional. Provides direct LAN access for live local polling and fast gateway writes — pairing handles authentication via RSA signing, no password required.
data_description.update_teslemetry_token: Paste a new token to rotate it. Leave blank to keep your existing token.

sigenergy_connection.title: Sigenergy connection

optimization.title: Smart Optimization settings
optimization.description: Choose the part of Smart Optimization you want to change. Everyday goals stay separate from occasional behaviour, battery specifications, and specialist constraints.

data_description.optimization_provider: Smart Optimization uses an LP solver to minimise costs. Native mode uses your battery's built-in TOU scheduler.
data_description.optimization_enabled: Turn the LP optimizer on or off without changing the saved Smart Optimization settings.
data_description.optimization_auto_apply_reserve: Preserves the selected minimum as a forecast buffer by adding expected home load between an export window and the next charge. Natural self-consumption may continue toward that buffer; Auto-Apply never lowers below your selected minimum or changes the hardware backup reserve.
data_description.optimization_ev_integration: Adds EV demand from PowerSync-managed charger schedules to Smart Optimization. Leave this off when the planned EV load forecast sensor already represents the same charging demand.
data_description.optimization_load_entity: Optional recorder-backed live household load sensor for Smart Optimization history. Use a no-EV load sensor here if your home load history includes EV charging and you also add planned EV load separately.
data_description.optimization_planned_ev_load_entity: Optional sensor with a planned_load attribute for forecast-only EV demand. PowerSync always adds this sensor to the load forecast when selected, but does not control the charger.
data_description.monitoring_mode: Block battery and inverter control commands while still updating prices, sensors, and plans for observation.
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will not intentionally export battery energy below this percentage. Natural self-consumption may continue to the separate hardware backup reserve. Default 20% is a good starting point.
data_description.hardware_backup_reserve: The battery's own backup reserve for grid outages. PowerSync restores this level after temporary hold or force-control modes.
data_description.optimization_battery_capacity_wh: Total usable battery capacity in kWh. This should match the battery specs shown in the mobile app.
data_description.optimization_max_charge_w: Maximum grid charge power in kW that the LP optimizer may request.
data_description.optimization_max_discharge_w: Maximum battery/inverter discharge power in kW that the LP optimizer may request for loads and export.
data_description.optimization_max_grid_export_w: Site/DNSP export cap in kW. Leave blank to auto-detect where available; set 0 for zero-export sites. This does not limit battery power used by house loads.
data_description.optimization_max_grid_import_w: Maximum total site grid import in kW. Set 0 for no site import cap; solar can still add charging power above this limit.
data_description.optimization_max_grid_charge_price: Advanced: never plan forced battery charging from grid import above this import price. Set 0 to disable the price cap.
data_description.optimization_grid_charge_soc_cap: Advanced: stop forced grid charging once forecast battery SOC reaches this cap. Solar surplus can still charge above the cap.
data_description.optimization_allow_grid_charge: When disabled, Smart Optimization can still charge from solar surplus but will not plan forced battery charging from grid import.
data_description.optimization_spread_export_enabled: When enabled on supported batteries, Smart Optimization spreads planned battery export across the full eligible export window instead of using maximum discharge power.
data_description.optimization_spread_import_enabled: When enabled on supported batteries, Smart Optimization spreads planned grid charging across each same-price import window instead of using maximum charge power.
data_description.optimization_disable_idle: Replace ordinary Smart Optimization idle hold actions with self-consumption for any electricity provider. Holds required to meet an active Charge By Time target are preserved.
data_description.profit_max_enabled: Let Smart Optimization prioritise profitable export opportunities instead of holding battery charge for later by default.
data_description.charge_by_time_enabled: Force Smart Optimization to reach the configured target SOC by the configured time.
data_description.charge_by_time_target_time: HH:MM time for Charge By Time to reach the configured target SOC. Default 17:15.
data_description.charge_by_time_target_soc: Battery SOC Charge By Time should target by the configured time. Default 100%.

network_export.title: Network export limits / Flexible Exports
network_export.description: Read a network operating envelope already enforced by certified site equipment. PowerSync is not a CSIP-AUS client and cannot replace, disable, or override SAPN controls. This release is monitoring-only while the required SAPN site soak is completed.

data.network_export_all_der_attested: All exporting DER is covered by the approved limiter

data_description.network_export_mode: Off ignores the source. Monitoring displays the envelope and suppresses intentional PowerSync export. Active control will be enabled only in a later release after the required SAPN site soak.
data_description.network_export_limit_entity: Select the original read-only W or kW entity exposed by certified inverter, gateway, or vendor equipment. Templates and PowerSync entities cannot arm Active mode.
data_description.network_export_status_entity: Optional source status entity. Invalid, unavailable, expired, error, or offline states make the live envelope invalid.
data_description.network_export_expiry_entity: Optional timestamp entity for the live envelope's expiry.
data_description.network_export_schedule_entity: Optional entity with a schedule attribute containing start, end, and limit_w entries.
data_description.network_export_pcc_power_entity: Current aggregate grid/PCC power in W or kW, using positive import and negative export. Required for Active mode.
data_description.network_export_scope: Use aggregate connection-point scope for active multi-phase sites. Per-phase envelopes remain monitoring-only.
data_description.network_export_fallback_limit_w: Fixed export limit in watts from the connection agreement, used when the live envelope is stale or invalid. Zero is valid.
data_description.network_export_safety_margin_w: Extra headroom below the effective limit in watts. PowerSync always enforces at least 250 W or 5%, whichever is larger.
data_description.network_export_site_phase_count: Number of phases at the connection point. Multi-phase Active mode requires an aggregate source.
data_description.network_export_all_der_attested: Confirm only when every battery and inverter capable of export is included in the approved whole-site control path.

amber_options.title: Amber Electric options
amber_options.description: Configure TOU sync, export boost, spike protection, and curtailment. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

data_description.inverter_entity_prefix: Prefix used by GoodWe Experimental entities. For sensor.goodwe_pv_power, enter goodwe.
data_description.update_amber_token: Paste a new Amber API token to rotate it. Leave blank to keep your current token.
data_description.auto_sync_enabled: When enabled, PowerSync uploads a new TOU tariff schedule to your battery every 5 minutes based on current Amber prices.
data_description.amber_forecast_type: Predicted is balanced. Low assumes cheaper prices (more aggressive charging). High assumes expensive prices (more conservative).
data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery from exporting to grid when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh. This is the main toggle -- must be enabled for any curtailment to work.
data_description.spike_protection_enabled: When Amber detects a price spike, artificially inflate buy prices to prevent your battery from charging from grid.
data_description.ac_inverter_curtailment_enabled: Shut down a separate AC-coupled or string solar inverter (Fronius, Sungrow, GoodWe, Enphase) when export earnings drop below 1c/kWh. Do not enable this for the hybrid battery inverter already configured as the battery system.
data_description.forecast_discrepancy_alert: Send a mobile notification when Amber predicted and High (higher-price) forecasts diverge significantly. Indicates the forecast spread is large and conservative scheduling may be preferable.
data_description.forecast_discrepancy_threshold: Average price difference between Amber predicted and High forecasts that triggers the alert. Default 10 in the selected price unit.
data_description.force_tariff_mode_toggle: After uploading a new tariff, briefly toggle operation mode to force immediate recalculation. Tesla only, causes a ~5 second interruption.
data_description.export_boost_enabled: Artificially increase sell prices during configured hours to encourage battery export during high-value periods.
data_description.export_price_offset: Amount added to the actual Amber export price. Example: Amber 18c + Offset 5c = battery sees 23c.
data_description.export_min_price: Floor price after offset. Example: Amber 12c + Offset 5c = 17c, bumped to Floor 20c.
data_description.export_boost_threshold: Only apply boost when the actual Amber price (before boost) is at or above this value. Use 0 to always boost.
data_description.chip_mode_enabled: Suppress exports during configured hours unless price exceeds threshold. The inverse of export boost.
data_description.chip_mode_threshold: Allow exports only when the price is at or above this value during chip mode hours.

data_description.globird_zerohero_end: Only used when GloBird plan is ZeroHero custom.
